outcome: `BANDIT-053` landed and closed out the bootstrap-gap chore for `BANDIT-GAP-AGENT-OBSERVABILITY-TRACES`. The work defines a repo-native Agent Observability Trace policy, validator, representative trace fixture, and derived non-canonical projection for agent runtime behavior, including operation spans, work item and stage correlation, source-artifact links, retry/failure/latency/token signals, external-payload data-only handling, and explicit refusal to treat traces or projections as canonical workflow artifacts. Stage 4 scoped pre-PR CodeRabbit passed after a focused validator repair, Local Qwen returned `non_blocking` findings that Codex PM dispositioned with no new chore, Stage 5 landing verdict and local-record landing action evidence are recorded, explicit layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence are recorded, and no full telemetry backend ingestion, hosted observability integration, broad dashboard UI, Evidence SLO policy, token-cost failsafe policy, Stage Capability Scope enforcement, scheduler execution, worktree lifecycle authority, claim lease work, work-surface reservation work, cockpit UI/server/API work, PR/CI workflow, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT scope, live reviewer routing change, paid reviewer route, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work was introduced.
what_worked: The chore kept observability at the repo-native policy, validation, trace fixture, and derived projection boundary instead of introducing a telemetry backend or cockpit authority.

Focused RED and implementation evidence covered trace-policy parsing, operation span required fields, correlation, non-canonical projection, canonical-artifact refusal, and input-quarantine refusal paths.

Stage 3 implementation preserved the Bootstrap Model-Family Separation and Test Ownership Boundary while keeping scheduler execution, claim/worktree authority, token-cost policy, Evidence SLO policy, and cockpit UI/server/API behavior out of scope.

Stage 4 review found useful validator hardening issues through CodeRabbit, then accepted the repaired policy-driven validator with current pre-PR CodeRabbit pass evidence and Local Qwen non-blocking disposition.

Stage 5 landing used current review-subject hash evidence, layered risk-classification evidence, supply-chain gate evidence, land-check, auto-land-check, and local-record landing action without expanding workflow authority.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Agent runtime observability needs first-class traces, operation spans, and correlation metadata while repo artifacts remain canonical workflow state.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `BANDIT-053` adds the Agent Observability Trace policy, validation command, projection command, representative trace evidence, focused tests, implementation evidence, review evidence, Stage 5 landing verdict, local-record landing action, and Stage 6 closeout; `.bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json` marks `BANDIT-GAP-AGENT-OBSERVABILITY-TRACES` resolved during closeout.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Observability projections are useful only if they are explicitly derived and non-canonical.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: The implemented validator and projection report `derived_non_canonical` authority and fail closed if traces or projections claim they can satisfy required workflow artifacts, approvals, gates, roadmap/current-context state, claim authority, coordination history, UAT, landing, or retrospective evidence.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Trace-backed token and cost signals should be collected as observability evidence before recurring spend policy is defined.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `BANDIT-053` records token, cost, latency, retry, and failure signals as trace/projection data only. Provider-pricing evidence, spend-class approvals, abnormal-run budgets, retry budgets, and continuation decisions remain queued under `BANDIT-GAP-TOKEN-COST-FAILSAFE`.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Stage capability and tool scoping still need enforcement in work-item specs and validation.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `BANDIT-GAP-STAGE-CAPABILITY-SCOPE` remains the next queued bootstrap gap after this closeout and should become `BANDIT-054` before unrelated Phase 8 cockpit work.

lessons_and_dispositions.lesson: Local Qwen's accepted type-hygiene and trace-level source-artifact concerns were real but not blocker-level for this narrow validator.
lessons_and_dispositions.rationale: `docs/work/BANDIT-053/qwen-finding-disposition.md` records why the local `readJson` helper and trace-level `source_artifacts` model are acceptable for this scope, and why the missing-directory/empty-trace concern is already resolved by existing code and evidence.
